title: Drop Mixing
description: Cut to a new track exactly on the drop — the most impactful transition in electronic music.

requiredTracks.characteristics: tracks with clear build-ups and drops — EDM, techno, or house

steps.instruction: Watch: Deck A is in its build-up. The tension is rising. Deck B is beatmatched and ready.
Watch: at the exact moment of the drop, the crossfader cuts hard to Deck B. The new track explodes in.
Watch: Deck A fades out immediately. Deck B is now at full volume with its drop in full effect.

phases.instruction: A drop mix is a surgical strike. You do not blend — you cut. The crowd does not expect it. One moment they are in Track A's build-up, the next they are inside Track B's drop. The key is absolute precision on timing — a beat late kills the effect.

steps.instruction: Beatmatch Deck B to Deck A. For a drop mix, the BPMs must be exact — within 1 BPM.
Cue Deck B to its drop — the first beat after the build-up. Use the Cue button to mark that point.
Listen to Deck A. It is building up. Count the bars — how many until the drop? Anticipate the moment.
One bar before the drop — start Deck B playing from its cue point. It plays silent in the background, aligned.
On the exact first beat of the drop — snap the crossfader all the way to Deck B. Quick. Decisive. On the one.
Kill Deck A's volume immediately. Do not leave it lingering — the cut should be clean.
Listen to Deck B's drop playing. Did it hit on time? A perfect drop mix feels like one continuous track, not a switch.
